question: B-002-audit-linkevolution
claim: Edge-granularity versioning: RELATES_TO carries valid_from/valid_to + ingested_at/expired_at; ONE current edge per ordered (source,target); a different label = close-and-replace revision so a pair can't fork into two current links. code_edges has NO temporal columns (LWW on reindex). Distinct from Q1-C2 (node rename) and Q1-C1 (store reindex).

question: B-002-audit-linkevolution
claim: Every governance op (promote/demote/revise/refuse) leaves an AuditEvent queryable by subject_id, by kind, in time order, wired to the affected entity (AuditEvent-AUDIT->Note). Internal tombstones record ONLY deletions (env-gated, pruned to 100); provenance is a single global snapshot, not per-decision/per-subject queryable.

question: B-002-audit-linkevolution
claim: Confidence floor + per-run caps on links created/revised/distinct-notes-affected + a per-pair revision cap counted from closed versions, so a flip-flopping relationship can't be rewritten without bound. Internal heuristic edges (TESTED_BY@0.6, CALLS@0.8) are written every scan with no write-time churn bound.

question: B-002-audit-linkevolution
claim: Single funnel owns dedup; on a dedup hit a one-way latch only UPGRADES (blank->signed), never strips/downgrades; whichever copy lands first must not shadow the better one. Internal edge dedup DROPS rather than reconciling attributes (no keep-higher-confidence rule).

question: B-002-audit-linkevolution
claim: Closed relationship vocabulary enforced by the driver CHECK 'even if a future evolver does not'; OOV labels dropped (anti-drift/anti-injection). edge_type is TEXT NOT NULL with no CHECK, while parser_skip_list.error_class already uses CHECK(...IN(...)) — pattern exists, just not applied to edges.

question: B-002-audit-linkevolution
claim: Keyring-anchored signing, per-row verdict computed at read (valid/unsigned/invalid/untrusted/not-checked), tampered row reads invalid without failing the query. No crypto signing exists (db.ts:172 signature is the CODE function-signature text). FLAG: likely over-engineering for a local single-writer code graph.

question: B-002-audit-linkevolution
claim: Coherent edge-governance cluster: edge-bitemporal-lifecycle (edge-granularity, fills the gap between Q1-C1 store-level and Q1-C2 node-level) + cascade-guard + closed-vocab CHECK. Per-row crypto-signing flagged over-eng for local single-writer. Open: only heuristic edges get versioning, or structural too? newInfoRatio 0.80.
